alignments.candidate_evidence: 8 years at Google in Strategy and Operations / Product Operations with two promotions; core positioning is Strategy and Operations leader
alignments.job_requirement: Strategy & Operations department role reporting to CEO Office

alignments.candidate_evidence: Experience scaling global commercial operations and transformation programs at Google Devices and Services
alignments.job_requirement: Strategic cross-functional scope and transformation leadership

alignments.candidate_evidence: Product operations ownership, requirements definition, transformation program execution, and cross-functional stakeholder management demonstrated at Google
alignments.job_requirement: Role requires clear ownership and ambition-setting capability

gaps.gap: JD severely corrupted and incomplete—no readable scope, responsibilities, team size, reporting relationship, or qualifications visible
gaps.mitigation: Request clean JD copy from source URL or recruiting contact to enable proper role-level and requirement mapping

gaps.gap: Payments/fintech domain background not evident in Google Devices and Services background
gaps.mitigation: Candidate has proven ability to learn new domains through transformation and product operations work; fintech is learnable but adds ramp time

gaps.gap: CEO Office roles typically require proven high-stakes cross-functional influence and executive alignment; candidate evidence focuses on product/ops execution rather than C-suite advisory scope
gaps.mitigation: Clarify role scope—if true Chief of Staff / strategic advisor role, assess whether candidate has led enterprise-wide transformations or board-level initiatives

output.level_rationale: JD is corrupted and unreadable. Title "Strategy, CEO Office" at an unknown-stage fintech suggests L5–L6 scope (5–8 years or senior-IC strategy ownership), but without JD text, team scope, reporting clarity, or company stage, confidence is low. CEO Office roles are often L5–L6 at scale-stage companies; interpret cautiously pending clean JD.
output.summary: Strong family fit (Strategy & Operations / Chief of Staff) and 8-year tenure align well, but the JD is severely corrupted—no readable scope, responsibilities, reporting line, or requirements. Estimated level L5 based on title alone. Cannot assess scope seniority, CEO-office-specific influence scope, or fintech domain fit without legible JD. Recommend obtaining clean job description before proceeding.
uncertainties: JD source is severely corrupted HTML / CSS / JavaScript with no readable job description text beyond title and location
Company stage and size unknown; cannot calibrate 'Strategy, CEO Office' level expectations
Reporting relationship, team scope, and strategic charter entirely unclear
CEO Office roles may imply executive-level stakeholder management and board exposure; candidate evidence emphasizes product/ops execution rather than C-suite advisory
